evals.prompt: Help me draft the 50-character pitch and the 'what is your company going to make' answer. My company is an AI tool that auto-generates legal contracts for SaaS companies. We have 12 customers paying $200/mo. Two technical co-founders, both ex-Stripe.
evals.expected_output: Two crisp pitch variants under 50 chars. 2-4 sentence product description with X-but-Y framing or matter-of-fact concrete description. No buzzwords.

evals.prompt: Critique this answer to 'most impressive thing': 'Our team has decades of combined experience building world-class products at top tech companies. We are exceptionally driven and uniquely positioned to win this market.'
evals.expected_output: Flag every adjective. Identify it as exact pattern Paul Graham warns against. Ask for specific verifiable achievements. Offer rewrite pattern.

evals.prompt: I got a YC interview next Friday for the Summer 2026 batch. What should I do to prepare?
evals.expected_output: Reference interview-prep.md. Tell them NOT to over-prepare. Highest leverage = ship something between now and interview. Provide question bank. Numbers cold. 10-min format.

evals.prompt: I applied last batch with the same idea and got rejected. Should I reapply this batch?
evals.expected_output: Pull from reapplying.md. Conditional: reapply if material progress shipped, else wait. Explicit guidance on 'what changed' question. Honest framing.

evals.prompt: What's a YC RFS and should I tailor my application to one?
evals.expected_output: Explain RFS = Requests for Startups. Don't reverse-engineer company to match. Mention if natural fit. Reference yc-context.md. Don't fabricate Spring 2026 list.
